content:
Is anyone interested in having an Option that is compatible with pin-init. So where one could store a Option<Mutex<T>>.

I have a half baked patch ready in https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/commit/fd00f9159eb8ccbd38f89203060c4443cf7b8efd, which I think I created before or after sending https://lore.kernel.org/all/DC73760NQA0X.29E82RNZCV3FS@kernel.org.

Anyway I still have it as a branch and would like to clean it up if there is no need for it.

I think I proposed a horrible trick/hack some time ago but couldn't find it written anywhere.

The idea is that if there's no niche,Option<T> should be the same, layout-wise, as Option<MaybeUninit<T>>, which could be safely initialized. If there is niche, then you can prepare a T and pretend it is Some(T)
how can you safely initialize Option<MaybeUninit<T>> without a move?
this is also about the pinning invariant of Option, I don't think the standard library is making any guarantees in either direction here
I forgot to mention, I cross posted this on the mailing list too: https://lore.kernel.org/all/DH5PZ4EWAFK0.1R0BVA8N1GK28@kernel.org

I have a structure with a huge optional field (which gets initialized much later after structure creation) and there doesn't seem to be a nice way to handle this with Option. It does not need to be pinned (at least I hope so) though. Having something for this would be useful
can you share a link if the code is publicly available?

It is not yet, I'm currently struggling with stack overflows. But the driver can already barely communicate with the device. The code is quite messy, I've attached a simplified version. I had to make the QueueHandler much smaller. I couldn't figure out any way to make rustc not do the stupid thing of allocating 30k on the stack, so I had to make the QueueHandler much smaller. At least this lets me keep going.
virtio-gpu.rs
For the reference, this will be a VirtIO-GPU driver for some unnamed OS.

ah you're not working on something in the Linux kernel. When you're using the init! macro, you're not pinning your data. So in order to use the pinned option, you'd have to annotate your structs with #[pin_data] and then use pin_init! instead.

Depending on how much I have going on next cycle, I'll add the pinned option then
Yeah, I guess here having an Option<T>with impl Init<T> initialization would be better, similarly to what Box has. I guess there are no plans for something like this, right?

there is no way to write that function today, see https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/pin-init/issues/59 for more information. Essentially you cannot unsafely write an enum variant to a pointer field-by-field.
we would have to have this feature: https://github.com/rust-lang/rfcs/pull/3727
I can give you a separate initializer for PinnableOption that takes a impl Init<T> and returns an impl Init<Self> instead of pinned initializers

What I have in mind is more like PinnableOption::none().write_init(...). The big_struct_in_place example seems to focus on the struct ending up in a Box, but here struct is an optional field of some another boxed struct that just has to be initialized later in-place. Currently the only way to initialize something in-place is to use the (internal?) __init method of the impl Init<T>. I need to experiment with this a bit more though. This probably deserves a separate topic...

Benno Lossin said:

how can you safely initialize Option<MaybeUninit<T>> without a move?

Right, x = Some(MaybeUninit::uninit()) doesn't get folded without opt level
